title: RLBench -> IsaacSim Task Migration
description: Survey holder retained; the 5-task pilot has been promoted into the Self-improving AgenticSim verifier gate.
summary: Survey-stage migration of the Notion idea to move RLBench 100+ manipulation tasks into Isaac Sim / Isaac Lab: decide whether a small pilot can preserve task semantics, demonstrations, observations, rewards, and VLA evaluation value while gaining GPU-scale simulation and richer sensors.

details.text: RLBench provides a large CoppeliaSim/PyRep manipulation benchmark with over 100 hand-designed tasks and expert demonstrations. The Notion idea is not to copy it mechanically, but to reuse the task library as a source of structured manipulation scenarios for the self-improving simulator loop.

Isaac Lab / Isaac Sim adds GPU-scale robot learning infrastructure, richer rendering/sensors, and modern policy-training workflows. The open question is whether task scripts, assets, rewards, and demonstrations can be converted with enough fidelity to justify the migration cost.

details: Pilot scope: pick 5 tasks across reaching, door/drawer, tool use, multi-stage placement, and contact-rich insertion; map observations, actions, rewards, object assets, demos, and success checks before discussing a full 100-task port.

Connection to dashboard: this is a Survey project because it can feed Self-Improving Agents / AgenticSim and VLA evaluation, but it should not consume engineering time until a pilot proves migration value.

Mainline merge 2026-06-05: execution moved to task_self_improving_rlbench_isaacsim_pilot_gate. The accepted route is ReachTarget smoke, PickAndLift formal verifier, then OpenDrawer, StackBlocks, and InsertOntoSquarePeg with explicit Isaac-side evidence.
risks_decisions: The main risk is silent semantic drift: an Isaac version of a task can look similar while changing contacts, rewards, camera geometry, or expert-demo assumptions.
Do not start with all tasks. A 5-task pilot should expose asset conversion, robot model differences, reward APIs, and demo replay blockers.
If the migrated tasks cannot produce dashboard-compatible evaluation traces or VLA inputs, keep this archived/reference-only instead of engineering it.

Next executable analysis: inventory RLBench task categories, select 5 pilot tasks, and write a conversion matrix covering assets, controllers, observations, rewards, demonstrations, and verifier criteria.
Survey disposition 2026-05-31: keep, because the idea is not done and is not covered by current dashboard cards; it has a clear but unvalidated route into Self-Improving Agents.

Mainline merge decision 2026-06-05: standalone survey matrix is closed; do not mark the new Self-improving task done unless there is Isaac-side verifier evidence or a reproducible environment blocker.
asset_alt: RLBench to Isaac Lab migration pilot diagram with task conversion matrix and semantic verifier
asset_caption: Survey diagram: five-task migration pilot from RLBench demos to Isaac Lab rollout and verifier checks.
